commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair lane is needed; the PR has no concrete fix blocker and is waiting on normal maintainer review and checks.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain concern was found; the diff stays within local process discovery, localhost probing, and tests without new dependencies, permissions, or secret handling. (sha=ed9f79d7964db5f8aab50e8f5b12f882c403c761)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an Antigravity CLI fast path that reuses an external warm `agy` language server before falling back to the existing managed spawn flow, exposes process discovery internally, and adds focused reuse/fallback tests.

Reproducibility: no. independent high-confidence live failure reproduction was performed because this was a read-only review and AGENTS.md warns against unrequested live provider probes. Source inspection shows current main always enters the managed spawn/session path with a 5-second readiness deadline, and the PR body provides live Linux timing evidence for the warm-reuse mitigation.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair lane is needed; the PR has no concrete fix blocker and is waiting on normal maintainer review and checks.

target.title: feat(antigravity): reuse a running agy language server for CLI usage
target.body:
## Problem

`codexbar usage --provider antigravity --source cli` spawns a fresh `agy` language
server on every one-shot invocation. A fresh server binds its port quickly, but
`GetUserStatus` returns transient initialization failures for the first few seconds,
and the readiness deadline is 5s. A one-shot CLI fetch therefore sits right at that
5s boundary with little margin — under load or on slower Linux hosts it can tip over
into "endpoint not ready".

When CodexBar runs long-lived (the app, or `codexbar serve`), the warm session is
reused and this margin problem disappears. The gap is specific to the one-shot CLI path.

## Solution

Before spawning, discover an already-running authenticated `agy` CLI language server
via the existing `detectProcessInfos(.ideAndCLI)` and reuse its listening ports through
the token-less `cliHTTPS` endpoint. If no warm server is found — or none answers with
parseable usage — fall through to the existing spawn path unchanged.

- IDE/app servers require a CSRF token and are excluded; only the `agy` CLI's
  token-less server is reused.
- CodexBar's own session-managed `agy` (tracked via `AntigravityCLISession.shared.pid`)
  is excluded from the warm scan, so reuse never bypasses `beginProbe`/`finishProbe`.
- "Warm if present, otherwise current behavior" — it never assumes a warm server exists.

The 5s deadline and the spawn path are unchanged; the spawn body was pulled into
`fetchBySpawning` in a separate refactor commit so the feature commit is purely additive.

## Real-world measurement (Linux)

Built `CodexBarCLI` for Linux (Swift 6.2, Ubuntu 24.04, x86_64) and measured against a
live authenticated `agy` language server, 10 runs each:

| | success | latency |
|---|---|---|
| **warm reuse** (this PR, warm `agy` present) | 10/10 | **~2.3s** |
| **spawn fresh** (warm absent → existing path) | 10/10 | ~5.0s (4.6–5.6s) |

Reuse log: `Antigravity CLI HTTPS reusing warm agy pid=… ports=…` → `Gemini Models: 99% left`.

Honest caveat: in this environment the spawn path did not actually *fail* (10/10), so I
can't claim a hard failure-rate reduction. What the data shows is that spawn-fresh
latency sits right against the 5s readiness deadline (4.6–5.6s) with essentially no
margin, while warm reuse completes in ~2.3s — roughly halving latency and restoring the
deadline margin that one-shot CLI calls otherwise lack, which is the mechanism behind
the intermittent "endpoint not ready" reports on busier/slower hosts.

## Tests

`Tests/CodexBarTests/AntigravityWarmAgyReuseTests.swift` (9 cases): warm reused without
spawning, IDE excluded, CodexBar-owned pid excluded (managed session untouched),
no-warm/fetch-failure → spawn fallback, and `fetchUsingWarmSession` via the `spawnFetch`
seam. `swift test --filter Antigravity` (210 pass), `make check` clean.
target.author: junmo-kim

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
